seoTitle: How to Scribble or Freehand Draw in Google Docs
metaDescription: Draw a quick sketch or freehand annotation in Google Docs using the built-in drawing canvas's scribble tool, no external app needed.
intro: Sometimes a quick freehand sketch communicates faster than typed text. This guide covers using Google Docs' built-in drawing canvas and its scribble tool to draw directly inside your document with a mouse, trackpad, or stylus.

steps.heading: Open the drawing canvas
steps.body: Click Insert, hover over Drawing, and select New to launch the interactive drawing studio.

steps.heading: Choose the Scribble tool
steps.body: Click the line dropdown in the toolbar and select Scribble to switch into freehand drawing mode.

steps.heading: Draw your sketch
steps.body: Click and drag your cursor or stylus across the canvas to create your freehand artwork or diagram.

steps.heading: Customize stroke color and thickness
steps.body: Use the border color and weight icons next to the scribble tool to adjust how your drawing looks.

steps.heading: Insert and align the drawing
steps.body: Click 'Save and Close' to place the sketch into your document, then set text wrapping to align it with nearby content.

faq.question: Can I edit a scribble after saving it?
faq.answer: Yes, double-click the inserted drawing to reopen the canvas and make further edits.

faq.question: Is a stylus required to scribble on Google Docs?
faq.answer: No, a mouse or trackpad works fine, though a stylus or touchscreen gives smoother results.

faq.question: Can I layer scribbles over an inserted image?
faq.answer: Yes, you can add an image to the same drawing canvas and scribble directly on top of it.

faq.question: Will the drawing print clearly?
faq.answer: Since drawings are vector-based, they print and export cleanly at most sizes.
